760414:
- Oh Hyeon-Gu came off the bench to score the winner in the 79th minute after Hwang equalized, so Korea pulled off a classic comeback against the Czechs

- Krejcí's header gave Czechia the lead out of nowhere since they barely had any chances all game, which made the turnaround even more dramatic

- Korea looked way sharper and had chances for days, so getting the W despite being down was basically them finally finishing their chances instead of wasting them like they did in the first half
760415:
- Mexico finally broke their curse and got a W in a World Cup opener after losing/drawing their last 7
- Wild match with three red cards, South Africa down to 9 men by the end; Jiménez and Quiñones scored in either half
- Good vibes for the co-hosts but Montes getting sent off late kinda killed the party
760416:
- Cyle Larin came off the bench and scored in the 78th to equalize after Bosnia went up 1-0 early, giving Canada their first-ever World Cup point on home soil
- The whole vibe was insane with Connor McDavid and Ryan Reynolds in a sea of "Ca-na-da!" chants at the CN Tower, so at least the crowd got something to celebrate
- Canada's still pretty rough at this tournament thing (only 2 goals ever at the World Cup) but at least they didn't lose this one
760417:
- Balogun went absolutely nuclear with a hat trick, including a sick third goal where he worked past two defenders and curled it top corner
- Paraguay gifted them one early when their own guy (Bobadilla) put it in the net, and USA just never let up after that
- Pochettino's squad looked dangerous at home; this is the kind of statement win that makes you think they could actually do damage in the tournament
760418:
- McGinn got a lucky deflection off a defender in the 28th and that was all Scotland needed
- Scotland's first World Cup win in 32 years and they're now top of the group after Brazil and Morocco drew
- Haiti still chasing their first-ever World Cup point since 1974, but their coach said one mistake and you're done
760419:
- Vinícius Jr. equalizes in the 32nd to rescue Brazil after Morocco came out swinging and scored first
- Brazil's fans went quiet early but got their vibe back when Vinícius threaded that perfect angled shot past the keeper
- Draw keeps Brazil's 21-game World Cup opener unbeaten streak alive, but they're gonna need way cleaner performances if they want that first title since 2002
760420:
- Qatar pulled off a dramatic late equalizer (own goal, technically) to snatch a draw after Switzerland's penalty gave them the lead
- The hosts nearly got humiliated on opening day but Khoukhi's header saved them at the last second
- Wild that there were tons of empty seats at a World Cup match; apparently people just weren't feeling it enough to sit in their actual seats
760421:
- Irankunda got the breakthrough early (27') with a clinical finish and became Australia's youngest-ever World Cup scorer at just 20
- Kid celebrated by punching the corner flag as a tribute to Tim Cahill, which was a pretty cool moment
- Australia's now tied with the US at the top of Group D, so that Seattle matchup on Friday is basically a mini final already
760422:
- Havertz went off with 2 goals as Germany demolished Curaçao 7-1, but the wild part was Curaçao actually tied it 1-1 early and had their fans losing it before Germany just took over
- After that goal, Germany was relentless and Nagelsmann said they needed that confidence boost heading into the tournament
- Pretty much a statement win to shake off whatever doubts people had, though it fell short of the all-time World Cup blowout record
760423:
- Amad Diallo scored in the 90th minute to snatch it 1-0, basically stealing the game at the last second
- Ecuador hit the woodwork THREE times and totally dominated the first half but somehow still lost; so frustrating for them
- Ivory Coast's 19-year-old Diomande was electric on the wing but they were sloppy in the final third until that clutch late goal bailed them out
760424:
- Yasin Ayari went off with a brace and Sweden just demolished Tunisia 5-1 in their opener, which is the kind of statement win you don't forget
- Isak shook off his rough Liverpool season to bag a goal and set up another, showing up when it mattered most
- Tunisia looked completely out of their depth after a perfect qualifying run; defensive mistakes and goalkeeper howlers made this a total mismatch
760425:
- Van Dijk put Netherlands up early in the second half but Japan equalized within minutes, then the Dutch went ahead again before Japan's late header forced a draw
- Japan's coach went all-in with five subs at once and it actually worked; they completely changed the game and got their equalizer in the 88th minute
- Netherlands probably should've won this but Japan showed they're not gonna roll over, making for a pretty wild back-and-forth match
760426:
- Lukaku came off the bench and literally the own goal happened 23 seconds later; that's the most Lukaku thing ever
- Egypt had them dead to rights but couldn't finish and now they're still winless at the World Cup in four tries
- Belgium's still in crisis mode with no wins in their last three tournament matches, so a draw feels like damage control
760427:
- Iran came back twice to grab a 2-2 draw after falling behind early, with Rezaeian and Mohebbi getting the goals
- The team had to fight through insane obstacles just to show up (visa issues, training venue changes, officials blocked from entering), and their fans showed up big despite FIFA trying to ban their flags
- They didn't get the win but definitely proved they belong on this stage, especially after all that drama just to get there
760428:
- Cape Verde held Spain scoreless in their World Cup debut; absolute madness for a team that was 1200-to-1 underdogs
- Their 40-year-old keeper Vozinha was insane, making huge saves and literally crying after the final whistle
- Even Lamine Yamal coming off the bench couldn't save Spain, and Cape Verde's whole country is probably losing their minds right now
760429:
- Saudi Arabia went up 1-0 early but completely fell apart in the second half and conceded late to blow what could've been another World Cup upset
- Uruguay was pretty flat for most of the game but just wore them down with pressure and finally got their equalizer with 10 minutes left
- Saudis had a chance to do the Argentina thing again but couldn't hold on; that's gotta sting
760430:
- Haaland finally got his World Cup goals with a brace, including one that literally bounced off the goalkeeper's clearance lmao
- Iraq actually equalized mid-way through but Norway just ran away with it, dominated the whole game
- Norway looking scary good right now, that attack is stacked with talent
760431:
- Austria finally broke a 36-year World Cup drought with a 3-1 win over Jordan, who were playing their first-ever World Cup game
- Wild second half had a disallowed goal (handball on Posch), then an own goal that actually counted, then a late penalty to seal it
- Austria looked shaky at times but Jordan couldn't capitalize, so this is huge for getting their campaign back on track after missing six straight tournaments
760432:
- Mbappé went off with a brace and broke Giroud's all-time scoring record for France (58 goals now); he's just different
- Senegal had them on their heels early with two chances that could've changed everything, but France completely took over in the second half
- Solid opener for Les Bleus but nothing that screams "this team's winning it all"; they'll need to tighten up against better opposition
760433:
- Messi went absolutely nuts with a hat-trick to tie Klose's all-time World Cup goal record (16), which is insane for a 38-year-old
- Argentina dodged a Saudi Arabia situation this time around; they got caught slipping in 2022 but came out swinging here with the 3-0 shutout
- Messi's still got juice left to break the record in their other group games, but he's gotta stay ahead of Mbappé who's hot on his heels
760434:
- Yirenkyi scored in the 95th minute off a cross from Thomas-Asante to steal it late and keep Panama winless
- Ghana's keeper got banged up early and had to come out at halftime, but the backup held it down when it mattered
- Ghana's top of the group now, but they were playing without Partey the whole time due to legal stuff back in England
760435:
- Ronaldo's Portugal couldn't break through against DR Congo and settled for a 1-1 draw, with João Neves scoring early before Yoane Wissa equalized right before halftime
- DR Congo's goal was historic; their first ever World Cup goal after getting absolutely demolished in 1974 (lost 9-0 to Yugoslavia lol), so this draw felt massive for them
- Huge upset vibes in the stadium as the underdog African team showed up and refused to get steamrolled, proving they actually belong at this tournament
760436:
- Muñoz got Colombia rolling early, but Uzbekistan actually equalized and made it a fight before Díaz and Campaz put it away in the second half
- Man City's Khusanov took out a cameraman getting a yellow card — physical match with Colombia's Mojica also booked in the first seven minutes
- Colombia finally got their World Cup started after being one of the last teams to play, and Uzbekistan showed they're not just here to get smoked despite being debutants
760437:
- England went absolutely wild in the second half to pull away 4-2, so Croatia couldn't hang on despite keeping it close early
- Classic end-to-end chaos with both teams finding the net multiple times — felt like a proper back-and-forth battle rather than a boring blowout
- England's finally looking dangerous again after some rough patches, and that attacking firepower could be scary in the knockouts
760438:
- Czechia went up early through Sadílek in the 6th minute, but South Africa equalized on an 83rd-minute penalty from Mokoena
- Mokoena was literally crying before the match thinking about his late grandfather, then scored the pen with his presence on his mind — pretty emotional stuff
- Both teams desperately needed points after losing their openers, so this draw leaves them both still alive but in a precarious spot
760439:
- Switzerland absolutely ran away with it in the second half after what must've been a pretty even first 45
- Bosnia got one back to make it 4-1 but honestly that was just a consolation at that point
- Swiss looked clinical and in control — this is the kind of dominant performance that makes you think they're legit contenders
760440:
- Canada absolutely demolished Qatar 6-0 at home with Cyle Larin and David (two goals) doing the damage early
- Qatar got down to nine men after two red cards, including one brutal tackle on Koné that looked like it broke his leg — pretty ugly stuff
- Home crowd went nuts and Canada got their first World Cup W in dominant fashion, though the Koné injury definitely put a damper on the celebration
760441:
- Luis Romo punished a massive goalkeeper howler in the 50th to seal it 1-0
- Mexico's keeper Rangel went full superhero mode with back-to-back saves in the 87th when it could've gone sideways
- Mexico's locked in as the first team through and gets to play the knockouts at home, which is basically cheating
760442:
- USMNT got the job done 2-0, pretty clean performance without needing to be perfect
- Felt like the kind of controlled win where the US just managed the game and didn't let Australia create much
- Good confidence builder heading into whatever's next, though it wasn't flashy enough to feel like a statement win
760443:
- Galarza scored early for Paraguay and they held on for the 1-0 despite Turkey dominating the second half
- Almirón got sent off in the first half for literally covering his mouth while talking trash — wildest red card ever
- Turkey needed this win badly after losing their opener and threw everything at it, but Paraguay's defense was clutch
760444:
- Cunha went off with two goals as Brazil absolutely demolished Haiti 3-0 in the first half and actually looked competent this time
- Raphinha had two goals ruled out for offside in like five minutes which was honestly hilarious, but Brazil didn't need the help anyway
- Haiti is basically done after back-to-back losses and Brazil's looking scary again after that snooze fest against Morocco
760445:
- Morocco grabbed the only goal and that was enough to send Scotland packing
- Scotland had chances but couldn't finish when it mattered — frustrating stuff
- Morocco's defense held firm the whole way, so they're looking legit in this tournament
760446:
- Eloy Room went absolutely nuclear with 15 saves (most ever in a 90-minute WC match) to pull off a shocking 0-0 draw for Curaçao
- After getting demolished 7-1 by Germany, this tiny island nation somehow scraped together their first-ever World Cup point and is still alive in the tournament
- They'll need to beat Ivory Coast to actually make it out of the group, which is basically asking for a miracle at this point
760447:
- Brobbey was an absolute steal of a pick, scoring twice in like 17 minutes and basically ending it before halftime even happened
- Sweden had a goal wrongly called back and then got completely run over in the second half when Gakpo went off for two more
- Netherlands just took over Group F with this demolition and honestly look way too good rn
760448:
- Both sides traded goals and neither could find a winner — Germany scored but Ivory Coast equalized to grab a point
- Classic situation where you feel like one team should've won it but the other had different ideas
- Feels like a "could've gone either way" result that probably left both teams wanting more
760449:
- Japan absolutely demolished Tunisia 4-0 with their best World Cup performance ever — Ueda bagged two and they were just way too fast
- Tunisia imploded so hard they fired their coach after one game, then brought in a new guy who couldn't stop the bleeding either
- Japan's basically through to the knockouts now and basically tied with the Netherlands, so they're back in it
760450:
- Cape Verde's Kevin Pina opened with an absolute rocket free-kick from 30 yards that somehow snuck past the wall and keeper
- Uruguay clawed back to lead at halftime but Cape Verde equalized in the second half after a defensive mistake, showing they're legit contenders
- This draw is huge for Cape Verde since they already got a point off Spain, so they're basically one result away from potentially advancing over a two-time champion
760451:
- Belgium's Nathan Ngoy got a straight red in the 66th for a stupid backpass and then fouling Taremi, basically handing Iran a golden opportunity
- Iran couldn't finish with the man advantage even though their keeper Beiranvand was solid with 7 saves, and Belgium actually created better chances down the stretch
- Both teams are now 0-2-1 in the group which is brutal, and Iran especially is gonna be kicking themselves for not punishing a top European team when they had the chance
760452:
- Salah finally broke through in the 67th and got a standing ovation when he came off — dude was the whole show out there
- Egypt's up top of the group but they're not through yet, gotta at least draw Iran on Friday to advance
- New Zealand scored first off a corner but couldn't hold it together, Egypt just wore them down
760453:
- Lamine Yamal scored 10 mins in on his first World Cup start and absolutely announced himself as a generational talent at 18 years old
- Spain needed this after that embarrassing 0-0 with Cape Verde, so demolishing Saudi Arabia 4-0 was the perfect response
- The kid's already being talked about as the next Messi/Ronaldo, so if he stays healthy he could genuinely be special
760454:
- Haaland went absolutely nuclear with two goals, putting Norway up 3-1 and joining some elite company (only second player in 50 years to score twice in each of his first two World Cup games)
- Senegal fought back to make it 3-2 but couldn't quite complete the comeback, so Norway got the W despite it being closer than it looked
- Dude's on a ridiculous streak right now — four goals in two games and he's basically carrying Norway's entire tournament
760455:
- Algeria pulled off the comeback with two corner goals in the second half after Jordan went up 1-0, which is honestly peak set-piece chaos
- Riyad Mahrez had a nightmare (multiple chances wasted) but his teammates bailed him out so it didn't matter
- Winner-takes-all vs Austria next for second place in the group — Algeria's still alive in this thing
760456:
- Messi missed a penalty early but then scored twice to set a new World Cup goal record (17 and 18 goals)
- He also broke the all-time wins record at the World Cup, so basically he's just breaking records at will
- Argentina's locked into the knockout stage already, so they're cruising through the group
760457:
- France grabbed the only goal and that was enough to get past Iraq in a pretty tight match
- It was a grind-it-out kind of game, not exactly thrilling but France found a way to close it out
- Solid defensive performance from France but they'll probably want to create way more chances next time out
760458:
- Kane had their best chance late but somehow skied the rebound after O'Reilly hit the bar — classic wasteful England moment
- Ghana's midfielder Thomas Partey got booed the entire match with his rape trial coming up next year, which hung over everything
- Massive comedown after the Croatia demolition — they basically sleepwalked to a 0-0 and still qualified, but Tuchel's side looked way less scary than they did six days ago
760459:
- Muñoz finally broke through late (second goal in two games) after their keeper Mpasi had been absolutely heroic all match
- Colombia came out absolutely firing with 8 shots in the first 20 minutes — just dominated early until the weather changed things up
- Colombia's basically through to the next round now, but Congo DR still has a shot if they beat Uzbekistan
760460:
- Ante Budimir scored early in the second half off a Stanisic cross, and that was all Croatia needed to get the W
- Panama had a legit chance to equalize in the 67th with three consecutive shots, but Livaković went full wall and shut them down
- Croatia's still alive for the knockout stage but can't afford to slip up against Ghana on Saturday
760461:
- Ronaldo bounced back with 2 goals after all the criticism from the draw, and he's now the first player ever to score in 6 different World Cups
- Portugal went nuclear and beat Uzbekistan 5-0 after everyone was worried about them following that rough opening match
- Martinez stuck with the 41-year-old and it totally paid off — Portugal just needed this win to get the campaign back on track
760462:
- Bosnia's 18-year-old Kerim Alajbegovic went off early with a banger from 20 yards to put them up 1-0 and became their youngest ever World Cup scorer
- Qatar got completely worked — an own goal in the 34th made it 2-0 and they basically never had a chance after that
- Bosnia's basically locked in for knockout round now which would be insane since they've only played in one World Cup ever before this
760463:
- Embolo was absolutely clinical in the second half, setting up both Swiss goals to secure top spot in the group
- Canada had chances to go through but couldn't finish when it mattered — Larin and Jonathan David both had solid looks that didn't go in
- Switzerland's heading home as group winners and gets a second game in Canada, while Jesse Marsch's squad has to make the road trip to LA for the knockout round
760464:
- Morocco went off for 4 goals but Haiti kept it competitive with 2, so it wasn't a total blowout even though the final score looks harsh
- Gotta be a wild match if Haiti managed to score twice against Morocco — they clearly came to play instead of just rolling over
- Morocco gets the W but probably didn't dominate as clean as they would've wanted given it took them four goals to put it away
760465:
- Brazil just dominated, Vinicius Jr and co. were basically playing a different sport out there
- Scotland couldn't get anything going and honestly looked completely outclassed for 90 minutes
- Pretty embarrassing result but at least they can move on and pretend this one didn't happen
760466:
- Maseko scored in the 63rd minute off a cross and that was enough — South Africa finally broke through the group stage after three straight failures
- South Korea's coach benched Son Heung-Min to start which was wild, brought him on at halftime but it didn't matter
- Now South Africa gets Canada in the knockouts while Korea sweats it out hoping to sneak in as a third-place team
760467:
- Mexico rotated their whole squad and still cruised 3-0, basically saying "we're already through, whatever" and it worked
- First half was basically a snoozefest until Mexico woke up and scored twice in six minutes to put it away
- Czechia's done — they're going home winless while Mexico's sitting pretty with a perfect group stage
760468:
- Sané put Germany up early but Ecuador just kept coming back — Angulo equalized quick and then Plata finished it off late to steal the W
- Germany had a penalty that would've put them up 2-1 in the second half but VAR saved Ecuador's ass by catching a foul in the buildup
- Ecuador's now topping the third-place rankings which is wild, and Scotland's basically watching their tournament hopes die in real time
760469:
- Nobody scored but honestly both teams were just happy to clinch knockout spots with a draw
- The expanded 48-team format made this a total snooze fest since third place could still advance anyway
- Australia locked in second place in the group and Paraguay probably through too, so it was mission accomplished for both sides
760470:
- Kaan Ayhan scored in the 8th minute of stoppage time to steal a 3-2 win for already-eliminated Türkiye — absolute heartbreaker for the US
- Pulisic made his return off the bench after a calf injury, but it didn't matter since the US had already clinched the group
- Wild that a meaningless game turned out to be the most dramatic — Türkiye finally got their W when it literally couldn't matter less for them
760471:
- Maeda put Japan up early but Elanga's left-footed banger equalized six minutes later to grab the draw
- Japan just set their all-time World Cup goal record with seven in the tournament, which is pretty wild
- Both teams advance but Japan gets Brazil in the knockout round while Sweden keeps their knockout streak alive
760472:
- Brobbey got his third goal of the tournament, but honestly the story was Tunisia just beating themselves with two own goals in the rain
- Van Dijk was basically playing midfielder with those headers into the box — Netherlands were just too much even though Tunisia was already eliminated
- Netherlands tops the group and gets Morocco next, while Japan somehow has to face Brazil after only drawing with Sweden lol
760473:
- Pépé went off with two goals to knock out the debutants and send Ivory Coast through as Group E runners-up
- Curaçao actually had a shot at advancing after getting a point vs Ecuador, but they just couldn't find the net when it mattered
- Ivory Coast will face France or Norway next, so they jumped straight into the deep end after finally breaking through to the knockouts
760474:
- Senegal absolutely demolished 10-man Iraq 5-0, with Pape Gueye bagging two in the second half after Iraq got a guy sent off early
- Iraq's defender got a red card in the 13th for taking down Mané and basically handed Senegal the game on a plate
- Senegal's still fighting for that third-place spot to make it through, but this domination definitely helps their goal differential case
760475:
- Dembélé went absolutely nuts with a first-half hat trick, including three goals in 25 minutes, which hasn't been seen at a World Cup since 1994
- Norway scored right after halftime to make it interesting for like 9 minutes before Dembélé just ended them with his third goal
- France is basically unstoppable right now with Dembélé on a rampage and Mbappé pulling strings, but losing Dembélé to injury in the 65th is the only real concern
760476:
- Egypt finally got out of groups (first time ever) thanks to an early Salah setup and Saber's goal, but needed Belgium to beat New Zealand to seal it
- Iran had a legit winning goal taken back for offside late and now has to sweat out whether they'll advance since they're no longer in control
- Pretty wild that neither team even wanted to play this "Pride Match" in Seattle but Egypt still got the better result
760477:
- Trossard went off with 2 goals as Belgium just demolished New Zealand 5-1 to top the group after looking shaky early on
- Wild turnaround considering Belgium were sitting third before this match with back-to-back draws, now they're suddenly looking dangerous
- New Zealand heads home winless at the World Cup while Belgium gets a shot at the round of 16 if they beat whoever they face next
760478:
- Cape Verde held Saudi Arabia scoreless again, making it three straight draws to somehow sneak into the knockout round as the smallest country ever to do it
- They've now got Spain, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia all tied up with either draws or comebacks — absolute madlad energy from a 500k population island nation
- Next up they're facing Argentina in Miami, which is basically the dream matchup that proves their coach right about nothing being impossible
760479:
- Baena scored off a Muslera howler in the 42nd — dude's now got 3 major mistakes this tournament and got subbed at halftime lol
- Uruguay's a two-time World Cup champ and they're going home winless, finished third and didn't even make the round of 16
- Players were actually crying on the field after, fans booed them walking off — brutal way to get eliminated
760480:
- Sučić absolutely ripped one from outside the box early (31'), then Vlašić headed in the winner in the 83rd to get Croatia back on track after a rough 2022
- Ghana equalized out of nowhere in the 73rd to make it tense, but their keeper couldn't come up with the clutch save when it mattered most
- Croatia's second place finish means they dodged England and stay alive in the tournament while Ghana at least made the knockout stage for the first time since 2010
760481:
- Nothing to see here — both teams already locked in their spots so it was basically a friendly with no stakes
- Bruno Fernandes almost broke through early but Vargas shut him down, and honestly neither team looked like they really cared that much
- Colombia tops the group and gets Ghana next while Portugal drops to runners-up and has to face Croatia, which is probably not ideal for them
760482:
- Yoane Wissa scored twice (including a penalty) and Fiston Mayele added one to complete Congo's comeback from down 1-0
- Absolutely mental turnaround in the second half after they'd already shocked everyone by drawing with Ronaldo's Portugal
- DR Congo made the knockout stage for the first time ever and only their second World Cup appearance after 52 years away — what a story
760483:
- Messi came off the bench to score his sixth goal in three games and now has 19 World Cup goals all-time, which is just ridiculous
- Argentina cruised to 3-1 with Lo Celso and Martinez scoring in the first half, so they wrapped up the group undefeated even with nine lineup changes
- Jordan at least left the tournament with some dignity, scoring in all three games despite losing this one
760484:
- Mahrez scored in stoppage time to put Austria out, then Kalajdzic immediately headed in the equalizer—absolute heart attack stuff
- Both teams basically agreed to advance together and dump Iran out, which is kinda wild when you think about it
- Austria's back in the knockout round for the first time since 1982 and gets Spain next, while Algeria faces Switzerland
760485:
- Bellingham and Kane both scored in the second half to put England away 2-0, with Kane breaking Gary Lineker's World Cup goal record
- Panama actually kept it scoreless through the first half and didn't get steamrolled, but England just flipped a switch after the break
- England's through to a better knockout bracket now with 7 points, so they basically got what they needed out of this one
760486:
- Eustáquio absolutely ripped a volley into the corner in the 92nd minute to break the tie and save Canada from extra time
- Dude plays for LAFC and basically scored the winner in his backyard with his own fans going nuts
- Canada finally got their first World Cup knockout win and advances to face Netherlands or Morocco
760487:
- Martinelli scored in stoppage time to seal it after Brazil looked like they were heading to extra time, absolute heartbreaker for Japan
- Japan actually went up 1-0 with a banger from Sano and nearly pulled off the upset, Brazil were kinda sluggish early
- Brazil scraped through but they'll need to play way better if they want to actually win this thing
760488:
- Morocco beat Netherlands on penalties after a 1-1 draw, with Ismael Saibari hitting the winning kick
- Cody Gakpo put Netherlands ahead late but Issa Diop equalized in the 90th to force the shootout — total momentum swings
- Morocco's through to the last 16 and the Dutch are going home, which honestly nobody saw coming at the start of this one
760489:
- Germany couldn't close it out despite dominating — Paraguay grabbed a late equalizer to spoil what should've been an easy W
- Wild that Paraguay fought back like that, definitely wasn't the expected result when Germany was rolling
- Pretty frustrating draw for Germany honestly, felt like dropping points they had no business dropping
760490:
- Haaland basically sealed it in the 86th with the go-ahead goal after Berg set him up wide open in the box
- Ivory Coast made it interesting when Amad Diallo equalized in the 74th, so Norway had to fight back after letting one slip
- Norway's heading to play Brazil in the round of 16, which is wild because they beat them 2-1 in the group stage back in 1998 — full circle moment
760491:
- Quiñones and Jiménez both scored in the first half to put Mexico up 2-0 and basically put the game away early
- Game got delayed an hour because of a crazy thunderstorm, but Mexico came out firing anyway with like 80K fans going nuts at the Azteca
- Ecuador never really threatened even though they had a couple chances, so Mexico's looking scary if England has to visit that stadium next
760492:
- Mbappé went off with 2 goals and is basically chasing down Messi's World Cup record at this point
- France just demolished Sweden 3-0 so they're looking scary heading deeper into the tournament
- Barcola got on the board too, meaning France's got multiple weapons and Sweden couldn't stop any of them
760493:
- Belgium pulled out a wild 3-2 win that definitely didn't feel comfortable given how close Senegal kept it
- Senegal came to play and made it a proper fight — this wasn't some easy cruise for the favs
- Honestly felt like Belgium scraped by rather than dominated, which is kinda sketchy if they wanna make a run
760494:
- Balogun had a masterclass first half with a goal and tons of chances, then got himself sent off in the 64th so the US had to finish it down a man
- Tillman sealed it with a free kick goal in the 82nd that went off the keeper's hand, which is basically the only way the US scores when they're playing 10v11
- This is only the US's second World Cup knockout win ever so beating Bosnia at home is actually kind of a big deal for getting them to face Belgium next
760495:
- Kane brace saved England from absolute disaster after going down early to DR Congo
- They looked shaky the whole match and got bailed out by luck more than anything, which is scary for a World Cup team
- First time winning after conceding since 1966, but Mexico next is gonna be rough if they play like this
